Joseph Arthur de Gobineau was a French aristocrat, diplomat, and writer. He is best known for his influential work, "An Essay on the Inequality of the Human Races" (also translated as "Essai sur l'inégalité des races humaines"), published in 1853-1855.

Gobineau's ideas about racial hierarchy and the superiority of the Aryan people have been widely criticized for their racist and pseudoscientific nature. His work has been associated with the development of Nazi ideology and has been used to justify racist and discriminatory policies.

It's worth noting that Gobineau's ideas have been largely discredited by modern scholars and are no longer considered a valid or scientifically supported theory.
